clinical_history: 30 year-old man admitted with coma. This ECG is most consistent with which of the following abnormalities?
diagnosis: This ECG shows classic signs of systemic hypothermia with sinus bradycardia (at about 52 beats/min), prominent J (Osborn) waves and QT prolongation (540 msec). The patient had a body temperature of 26.4 degrees C (79.5 degrees F).  He also had hyperkalemia (serum K+  6.9 mEq/L that may account for the prominent T waves superimposed on the hypothermia pattern and probably on an underlying physiologic early repolarization variant in this young adult man.  For comparison and further discussion, see Case #285.
